tasks.name: Add the newest fantasy novel under $18 to your main reading list
tasks.description: Locate fantasy books, filter to show only titles priced at $18 or less, sort by newest publication date, and add the single newest fantasy novel to the 'Main Reading List'. Success is achieved when exactly one book (the newest fantasy novel under $18) is added to the 'Main Reading List'.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the 'Books' option in the main navigation menu at the top of the page.
In the genre filter section on the Books page, open the genre dropdown and select 'Fantasy'.
Locate the price filter controls and set the maximum price field to '18', leaving the minimum price field unchanged.
Open the sort dropdown menu above the book results and select 'Publication Date: Newest First'.
In the updated list of fantasy books priced at $18 or less, click on the cover image of the first book in the results grid to open its detail page.
On the book detail page, click the 'Add to Reading List' button located near the main book information section.
If a reading list selection dialog appears, open the dropdown menu, select 'Main Reading List', and then click the 'Save' or 'Add' button in the dialog.

tasks.name: Favorite the longer of two mystery novels priced under $20 using the compare feature
tasks.description: Search for mystery novels under $20, add the first two results to a comparison view, determine which book has more pages, and add only the longer book to your favorites. Success is achieved when exactly one mystery novel under $20—the one with the higher page count out of the first two results—is added to your favorites list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the search bar in the header and type 'mystery', then submit the search using the Enter key or the search icon.
On the search results page, locate the price filter and set the maximum price to '20', leaving the minimum price field unchanged.
In the filtered results, click the 'Add to Compare' checkbox or button for the first book in the list.
Click the 'Add to Compare' checkbox or button for the second book in the list.
Click the 'Compare' bar or button that appears (usually at the bottom or top of the page) to open the comparison view for the two selected books.
In the comparison table, identify which of the two books has the higher page count by reading the 'Pages' row and deciding which number is larger.
Under the book with the higher page count, click the 'View Details' or similar button in the comparison view to open that book’s detail page.
On the selected book’s detail page, click the 'Add to Favorites' or heart icon button located near the book title or main action buttons.

tasks.name: Review a highly rated short work under 120 pages with a 4-star rating
tasks.description: Find a short work (such as a short story or novella) with a rating of at least 4.5 stars and fewer than 120 pages, then submit a 4-star review with a written comment. Success is achieved when one qualifying book has a new 4-star rating and a text review submitted through the on-site review form.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the 'Books' option in the main navigation menu.
In the format or type filter area, open the dropdown and select 'Short Story / Novella' (or equivalent short-form category).
In the rating filter, set the minimum rating to '4.5 stars' or select the checkbox for '4.5+ stars'.
Locate the length or pages filter and set the maximum pages field to '120', leaving the minimum pages field unchanged.
From the filtered results, click on the first book that appears in the list or grid to open its detail page.
On the book detail page, scroll to the reviews section and click the 'Write a Review' or 'Leave a Review' button.
In the review form, select a '4-star' rating using the star rating control.
In the text area of the review form, type a comment of at least one full sentence, such as 'I enjoyed this short story and especially liked the pacing and characters.'
Click the 'Submit Review' or 'Post Review' button at the bottom of the review form.

tasks.name: Register for the earliest online event in March 2025 and add a specific note
tasks.description: Use the events calendar to find online-only events in March 2025, select the earliest-dated one, and complete the registration form including a note that says 'Ask about sequel'. Success is achieved when the earliest online event in March 2025 has been registered for with the required note included in the form.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the 'Events' or 'Appearances' option in the main navigation menu.
On the events page, use the date picker or month navigation controls to switch the calendar to 'March 2025'.
In the event type filter, select 'Online' or check the 'Online Only' checkbox so that only online events in March 2025 are shown.
If there is a sort dropdown for events, set it to 'Date: Earliest First' or equivalent ascending date order.
From the filtered list of online events in March 2025, click the title of the first (earliest) event shown to open its event detail page.
On the event detail page, click the 'Register' or 'RSVP' button.
In the registration form, fill in the 'Name' field with any full name (for example, 'Alex Reader') and enter an email address in the email field (for example, 'alex.reader@example.com').
Locate the 'Notes' or 'Questions for the Author' field and type the text 'Ask about sequel' into that field.
Click the 'Submit Registration' or 'Register' button at the bottom of the form.

tasks.name: Create a 3-book sci-fi eBook bundle under $10 each and add it to the cart
tasks.description: Filter for science fiction eBooks costing less than $10 each, select the three highest-rated titles, add them to a custom bundle, and place that bundle in the cart. Success is achieved when a bundle containing exactly 3 science fiction eBooks, each priced under $10, is added to the cart.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the 'Books' option in the main navigation menu.
In the genre filter section, open the dropdown and select 'Science Fiction'.
In the format filter, choose 'eBook' so that only eBook formats are displayed.
In the price filter, set the maximum price to '10', leaving the minimum price field unchanged.
Use the sort dropdown above the results and select 'Rating: Highest First' to sort science fiction eBooks by rating from highest to lowest.
Click the 'Add to Bundle' button or checkbox for the first three books in the sorted list (top three highest-rated eBooks).
After selecting three books, click the 'View Bundle' or 'Bundle Summary' button or bar that appears on the page.
On the bundle page, make sure each of the three selected books has a quantity field set to '1' by typing '1' into any quantity field that is not already set to 1.
Click the 'Add Bundle to Cart' button on the bundle page.

tasks.name: Send a contact message about a bulk order of 25 paperbacks
tasks.description: Use the contact form to send a message about purchasing 25 paperback copies, selecting the 'Bulk Orders' topic and including a subject that references 25 paperbacks. Success is achieved when a contact form submission is made with the 'Bulk Orders' topic, a subject line mentioning 25 paperbacks, and a message body describing the request.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the 'Contact' or 'Contact the Author' link in the main navigation or footer.
On the contact page, open the 'Topic' or 'Reason for Contact' dropdown and select the option labeled 'Bulk Orders' (or the closest equivalent).
In the 'Name' field, type a full name such as 'Jordan Lee'.
In the 'Email' field, type an email address such as 'jordan.lee@example.com'.
In the 'Subject' field, type 'Bulk order for 25 paperbacks' exactly as the subject line.

In the main message text area, type a short message describing the request, including the number of copies and any timeline, for example: 'I would like to order 25 paperback copies of your latest novel for a reading group next month.'
Click the 'Send Message' or 'Submit' button at the bottom of the contact form.

tasks.name: Add the two cheapest highly rated young adult books from 2018–2020 to a 'Summer 2025' wishlist
tasks.description: Filter for young adult books published between 2018 and 2020 with a rating of at least 4.0 stars, sort by price to find the cheapest options, then add the two lowest-priced books to a wishlist named 'Summer 2025' (creating that wishlist if it does not already exist). Success is achieved when exactly two qualifying young adult books are saved in a wishlist labeled 'Summer 2025'.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the 'Books' option in the main navigation menu.
In the genre filter, select 'Young Adult' from the dropdown menu.
Locate the publication date or year filter and set the start date to 'January 1, 2018' and the end date to 'December 31, 2020'.
In the rating filter, set the minimum rating to '4.0 stars' or check the option for '4+ stars'.
Use the sort dropdown above the results and choose 'Price: Low to High' so books are ordered from cheapest to most expensive.
In the sorted results list, click the wishlist or heart icon for the first (cheapest) book and select the option to 'Add to Wishlist' or similar.

When prompted to choose a wishlist, either select an existing list named 'Summer 2025' from the dropdown or, if it is not listed, type 'Summer 2025' into the new wishlist name field and click the button to create or confirm it.
Return to the results list if necessary, then click the wishlist or heart icon for the second book in the sorted list and select 'Summer 2025' as the target wishlist from the dropdown.
Confirm the addition by clicking the 'Add' or 'Save' button in the wishlist selection dialog if one appears.

tasks.name: Save the most recent 'Writing Process' blog post with 5+ comments to your reading list
tasks.description: Browse the blog, filter for posts tagged 'Writing Process', identify the most recent post that has at least 5 comments, and save that single post to your reading list. Success is achieved when the newest 'Writing Process' post with a comment count of 5 or more is added to your reading list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the 'Blog' or 'Articles' option in the main navigation menu.
On the blog page, locate the tag or category filter and select the tag labeled 'Writing Process'.
Use the sort dropdown for blog posts and choose 'Newest First' or 'Most Recent' to order posts from newest to oldest.
Scroll through the list of 'Writing Process' posts and identify the first post whose comment badge or comment count indicator shows '5' or more comments.
Click the title of that post (the most recent one with at least 5 comments) to open its full article page.
On the article page, locate and click the 'Save to Reading List' or 'Add to Reading List' button, typically found near the top of the article or in a sidebar.
If a dialog appears asking where to save the article, choose an existing reading list from the dropdown (for example, 'Articles to Read') and click the 'Save' or 'Add' button.

entities.name: Book
entities.description: Single book entry in the catalog (one record per title/format combination) used for browsing, filtering, reviews, lists, bundles, cart, and comparisons.

fields.name: rating
fields.description: Average star rating from 0.0 to 5.0 used for rating filters and sorting

fields.name: rating_count
fields.description: Number of ratings used with rating to determine reliability

fields.name: page_count
fields.description: Total number of pages; used for comparison and short-work filters

fields.name: publication_date
fields.description: Publication date used for date-range filters and newest-first sorting

fields.name: is_featured
fields.description: Marks books that should be highlighted on the homepage

fields.name: keywords
fields.description: Array of strings for search indexing (e.g., ['mystery', 'detective'])

entities.name: Review
entities.description: Individual user-submitted review for a book, including star rating and text.

fields.name: rating
fields.description: Star rating value (1–5)

entities.name: LibraryList
entities.description: Generic list in the personal library, including reading lists, wishlists, favorites, and saved-article lists.

fields.name: name
fields.description: User-visible name, e.g., 'Main Reading List', 'Summer 2025', 'Articles to Read'

fields.name: is_default
fields.description: True for system-default lists like the main reading list or favorites

entities.name: LibraryListItem
entities.description: Item saved into a library list, referencing either a book or a blog article.

fields.name: bookId
fields.description: Set when item_type = 'book'

fields.name: articleId
fields.description: Set when item_type = 'article' (BlogPost)

fields.name: addedAt
fields.description: When this item was added to the list

entities.name: CompareSet
entities.description: A comparison context for books selected on the catalog page, shown side-by-side.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Optional label, e.g., 'current_compare'

fields.name: is_active
fields.description: True for the currently used compare set

entities.name: CompareSetItem
entities.description: A single book included in a compare set.

fields.name: addedAt
fields.description: When the book was added to the compare set

entities.name: Bundle
entities.description: Custom eBook bundle composed of multiple books before being added to the cart.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Optional bundle name shown in the cart

fields.name: total_price
fields.description: Cached sum of bundle item prices for quick cart display

entities.name: BundleItem
entities.description: A book included in a bundle with a specified quantity.

fields.name: quantity
fields.description: Number of copies of this book in the bundle (usually 1)

entities.name: Cart
entities.description: Shopping cart for the single user, containing individual books and/or bundles.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: When the cart was first created

entities.name: CartItem
entities.description: Item within the shopping cart, referencing either a single book or a bundle.

fields.name: bookId
fields.description: Set when item_type = 'book'

fields.name: bundleId
fields.description: Set when item_type = 'bundle'

fields.name: quantity
fields.description: Number of this item in the cart

fields.name: unit_price
fields.description: Price per unit at time of adding to cart

fields.name: total_price
fields.description: quantity × unit_price, cached for quick display

fields.name: addedAt
fields.description: When the item was added to the cart

entities.name: Event
entities.description: Author event or appearance, including online and in-person events shown in the calendar and list.

fields.name: start_datetime
fields.description: Event start date and time; used for month filtering and earliest-first sorting

fields.name: end_datetime
fields.description: Optional end date/time

fields.name: location
fields.description: Physical venue or description such as 'Online'

fields.name: online_access_link
fields.description: URL or instructions for joining online events

entities.name: EventRegistration
entities.description: Registration record for a specific event, capturing attendee details and notes.

fields.name: notes
fields.description: Optional attendee notes or questions such as 'Ask about sequel'

entities.name: BlogPost
entities.description: Blog article/post on the site, including writing process posts and others, with tags and comment count.

fields.name: published_at
fields.description: Publication date/time used for newest-first sorting

fields.name: tags
fields.description: Array of tag slugs, e.g., ['writing_process', 'craft']

fields.name: comment_count
fields.description: Cached number of comments used to find posts with 5+ comments

entities.name: Comment
entities.description: Comment on a blog post, optionally a reply to another comment.

fields.name: parentCommentId
fields.description: Optional ID of parent comment for threaded replies

entities.name: ContactMessage
entities.description: Submission from the contact form, including bulk order inquiries.
